message.content: Adversaries exploit weaknesses in service-related Registry permissions to hijack execution flow, enabling them to execute malicious payloads. By manipulating Registry keys, such as binPath/ImagePath, they can redirect service executables to their own code, achieving persistence and privilege escalation. This technique leverages improper access control lists and user permissions, allowing adversaries to modify service execution parameters using tools like PowerShell or Reg. They may also alter keys like FailureCommand to execute code in elevated contexts upon service failure or create new keys like Performance to load malicious DLLs. Mitigation strategies include enforcing strict Registry permissions, monitoring for unauthorized changes, and employing tools to detect and prevent Registry tampering.
